West.summary: Amsterdam West is a diverse residential area with good amenities. Parks like Erasmuspark and Rembrandtpark provide green space. Good tram and bus connections to the center.
concerns: Some areas can be busy during rush hour

De Baarsjes.summary: De Baarsjes is a popular neighbourhood in Amsterdam West, known for its multicultural character and proximity to parks. The area has undergone significant regeneration in recent years.

Oud-West.summary: Oud-West is a vibrant, trendy neighbourhood near the city center. Known for the Hallen food market and numerous bars and restaurants. Very well connected to the rest of Amsterdam.
strengths: Excellent food and cultural scene at De Hallen
concerns: Can be noisy, especially on weekends

Zuid.summary: Amsterdam Zuid is an upscale residential area with excellent amenities. Close to Vondelpark, Beatrixpark, and the Zuidas business district. High-quality housing stock and good schools.
strengths: Great public transport and road connections
concerns: Among the most expensive areas in Amsterdam
Can feel less lively than other neighbourhoods

Oost.summary: Amsterdam Oost is a diverse and family-friendly area with Oosterpark and Flevopark providing plenty of green space. Good value for money compared to more central neighbourhoods.
strengths: Oosterpark and Flevopark offer excellent green space
concerns: Longer commute to some parts of the city

Noord.summary: Amsterdam Noord is separated from the city center by the IJ river. Known for creative spaces, NDSM wharf, and more affordable housing. Ferry connections to central station are free and frequent.
strengths: Lots of green and open space
concerns: Some areas have fewer local amenities

Rivierenbuurt.summary: Rivierenbuurt is a quiet residential area in Amsterdam Zuid, close to the Amstel river and within cycling distance of the Zuidas. Known for its 1930s architecture and family-friendly character.
strengths: Good tram and train connections (RAI station)

Bos en Lommer.summary: Bos en Lommer is a rapidly developing neighbourhood in Amsterdam West. Close to Erasmuspark and with good access to both the ring road and public transport. Increasingly popular with young professionals and families.
strengths: Erasmuspark is a major green space nearby
Improving amenities and new businesses opening
concerns: Some areas still have limited local shops
Ongoing construction in parts of the neighbourhood

De Pijp.summary: De Pijp is one of Amsterdam's liveliest neighbourhoods, famous for the Albert Cuyp market. Extremely popular with students, young professionals, and expats. Dense urban living with everything on your doorstep.
strengths: Albert Cuyp market and excellent food scene
Very central, walking distance to museums
concerns: Very noisy, especially near the market
Among the highest rent in Amsterdam
